[The lords of quarters mentioned here are not the planets, but
are political functionaries viz. king, prince, leader, emissary, mer-
chant, spy, Brahmana (twice-born) and master of the royal
elephants. Vide LXXXVI-34, infra.]

A rainbow seen in the middle of water leads to drought; on

Signs of Rainbows XXXV 323
land, to destruction of crops; on a tree, to diseases; on an ant-hill,
to danger from weapons (or war); and at night, to the minister's
murder.
[Kalidasa alludes in his to this phenomenon of a rain-
bow being observed on the tip of an anthill—
u^n
A rainbow seen in the east when there is no rain, will produce
rain and vice versa; one seen in the west always indicates rain.
